overview:
## Return Value

A `CancellablePromise` with the following resolutions.

## Discussion

If the promise is successful, it will resolve with one of the following dictionaries:

The promise may reject and throw the following:

## Discussion

The `params` dictionary has the following properties:

If successful, the result contains a `CKDBRecordResponse` object with the created record. When creating a new record, if you omit the `recordName` property from `body`, the server assigns the record a UUID. When `assetType` fields exist in your record type, you must upload the assets prior to creating a record using the `createAssetUploadUrl` operation. For shared records, set the `createShortGuid` property in `body` to `true` in order to create a globally unique identifier for this record that the server uses when the share participant accepts a shared record.

Node.js example:

items.description: Accepts a share on behalf of the current user.
items.name: acceptRecord

items.description: Deletes records matching the provided query.
items.name: deleteRecordsByQuery

items.description: Fetches multiple records by record name.
items.name: lookupRecords

items.description: Returns records that changed since a specified sync token or since a zone was created.
items.name: queryRecordChanges

items.description: Returns a collection of records matching the provided query.
items.name: queryRecords

items.description: Fetches information about records given their shortGuid properties.
items.name: resolveRecord

title: createRecord
